title: Growth Mindset & Resilience
description: Build the mental framework to embrace challenges, learn from setbacks, and sustain long-term career growth.

modules.title: Understanding Mindset
modules.description: Learn the science behind fixed and growth mindsets and why it changes everything.

lessons.title: Recognising Your Fixed Mindset Triggers
lessons.content:
# Recognising Your Fixed Mindset Triggers

Everyone has a fixed mindset voice — the inner critic that says "I am not smart enough" or "I cannot do this." The goal is to recognise it, not silence it.

## Common Fixed Mindset Triggers
- Receiving critical feedback
- Watching someone else succeed in an area you care about
- Facing a challenge that feels beyond your current ability
- Making a mistake in public

## The Two-Step Shift
1. **Name the trigger** — "I am in fixed mindset right now because..."
2. **Reframe with a growth question** — "What can I learn from this?" / "What is the next small step?"

## The Power of "Yet"
Instead of: "I cannot do this."
Say: "I cannot do this **yet**."

This single word shift signals to your brain that the situation is temporary and changeable.

## Journal Prompt
Write about a recent fixed mindset moment. What triggered it? What would a growth mindset response have looked like?

modules.title: Building Resilience
modules.description: Develop the capacity to bounce back stronger from setbacks.

lessons.title: The Resilience Toolkit
lessons.content:
# The Resilience Toolkit

Resilience is not about never struggling — it is about recovering faster and growing stronger.

## The Four Pillars of Resilience

### 1. Meaning
People who find meaning in difficulty recover faster. Ask: "What is this teaching me? How will I use this?"

### 2. Connection
Isolation amplifies adversity. Lean on trusted people. Asking for help is a strength, not a weakness.

### 3. Perspective
Most setbacks feel permanent in the moment. Ask: "Will this matter in 5 years?"

### 4. Self-Care
Resilience runs on energy. Sleep, movement, and nutrition are not optional extras during hard times — they are the foundation.

## Processing Setbacks
1. Allow yourself to feel it — suppression delays recovery.
2. Separate facts from interpretations ("I did not get the job" vs. "I am a failure").
3. Identify one small action you can take today.
4. Revisit and learn once the emotional intensity fades.

lessons.title: Learning From Failure
lessons.content:
# Learning From Failure

Failure is data. The most successful professionals are not those who fail least — they are those who extract the most learning from it.

## The After-Action Review
Used by military organisations and high-performance teams. Apply it to any significant setback.

1. **What was supposed to happen?** (Your plan or expectation)
2. **What actually happened?** (The outcome)
3. **Why was there a gap?** (Honest root-cause analysis — avoid blaming others)
4. **What will I do differently next time?** (Concrete behaviour change)

## Normalise Failure in Your Narrative
- Most career stories include visible failures — redundancy, rejection, wrong turns.
- Reframing these as pivots or turning points makes them assets in interviews and conversations.

## The Failure Résumé
Some leaders maintain a "failure résumé" alongside their regular one — a record of their biggest setbacks and what they learned. It is a powerful exercise in normalising and owning your growth story.
